title: Scale CloudNative-PG PostgreSQL to 3 replicas with HA, PgBouncer, and S3 backups
subtasks: Update the CloudNative-PG Cluster CR to set `instances: 3` for high availability.
Configure synchronous replication and failover settings for production-grade HA.
Deploy PgBouncer as a sidecar or separate Deployment for connection pooling; update service endpoints to route through PgBouncer.
Configure automated daily backups and WAL archiving to S3/R2 (using the CloudNative-PG backup CRD and S3 bucket/credentials).
Test failover by simulating a primary outage and verifying automatic promotion.
Document connection string changes for all downstream services.

title: Scale Redis/Valkey to HA with Redis Sentinel and memory policy
subtasks: Update the Opstree Redis CR to deploy a Redis Sentinel cluster with 3 Redis replicas (1 master, 2 replicas).
Configure Redis Sentinel for automatic failover and high availability.
Set a maxmemory policy (e.g., allkeys-lru) and appropriate memory limits for production.
Test failover by killing the master pod and verifying Sentinel promotes a replica.
Update service endpoints and secrets as needed for Sentinel topology.

title: Configure TLS certificates for all services using cert-manager
subtasks: Install and configure cert-manager in the cluster if not already present.
Set up ClusterIssuer for Let's Encrypt (production) or internal CA as required.
Annotate all Ingress resources and Services with cert-manager certificate requests.
Ensure all public endpoints (frontend, APIs, Morgan agent) are served over HTTPS with valid certificates.
Rotate any self-signed or development certificates to production-grade certificates.
Test certificate renewal and expiry alerts.

title: Configure Cloudflare CDN and Ingress for static assets and public endpoints
subtasks: Verify Cloudflare proxy (orange cloud) is enabled for all public DNS records.
Configure CDN caching rules for static assets (JS, CSS, images) with long TTLs.
Set up Cloudflare Tunnel or Ingress for Morgan agent and any internal services needing external access.
Ensure all API and frontend endpoints are accessible via Cloudflare with HTTPS.
Test cache HIT/MISS headers and purge cache as needed.
Document CDN and Ingress configuration for future reference.

title: Apply strict Kubernetes NetworkPolicies for pod-to-pod traffic
subtasks: Define a default-deny NetworkPolicy for all namespaces.
Explicitly allow only required traffic between services (e.g., frontend to API, API to DB/Redis, Prometheus to /metrics endpoints).
Allow DNS and necessary egress for external API calls.
Test connectivity between allowed and denied paths using ephemeral debug pods.
Document the service communication matrix and policy rationale.

title: Configure OpenTelemetry Collector and Jaeger with persistent storage
subtasks: Deploy OpenTelemetry Collector as a Deployment or DaemonSet with production sampling rates (e.g., 1-10% for high-traffic endpoints).
Configure exporters for Jaeger and Prometheus.
Deploy Jaeger with persistent storage (e.g., using a StatefulSet and PVCs or external storage backend).
Update all service deployments to export traces to the OpenTelemetry Collector endpoint.
Test trace collection and retention by generating sample traffic and verifying traces are stored and queryable.
Document trace retention policy and storage sizing.

title: Define and apply Prometheus alert rules for SLO violations
subtasks: Define SLOs for each service (e.g., 99.9% availability, <500ms p95 latency, error rate <1%).
Write Prometheus alert rules for SLO violations (availability, latency, error rate, resource exhaustion).
Configure Alertmanager integration for paging/on-call notifications.
Test alert firing by simulating SLO breaches (e.g., inject latency, force errors).
Document all alert rules and escalation policies.
